response.justification: The candidate brings 15+ years of broad engineering experience, current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at a Staff level, and genuine high-throughput experience from Sociomantic (microservices handling millions of requests per second). However, Python — a must-have — is absent from the resume, the deepest systems work was done in DLang rather than the required stack, and there is limited evidence of directing technical strategy or managing cross-functional stakeholders at Staff scope.
strengths: Current AI-native experience building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling
Proven high-throughput, low-latency systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js background with solid craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No Python listed anywhere despite it being a must-have skill
Little demonstrated experience directing technical strategy or cross-functional stakeholder management at Staff scope
Deepest distributed-systems experience is in DLang and dates to 2018-2020, not recent or in the required stack
